goals: Expose a single, clean `vae_decode(vae, latent) -> PIL.Image` function in `pycomfy/vae.py`.
Match the pattern established by `sampling.py`: lazy ComfyUI import, typed public API,
Work correctly in CPU-only environments so CI passes without a GPU.
Be composable: `it_07 (ImagePipeline)` must be able to call `vae_decode()` directly

userStories.title: Decode a latent tensor to a PIL image
userStories.description: As a Python developer (or a higher-level pycomfy module), I want to call `vae_decode(vae, latent)` with the VAE object from `CheckpointResult` and the LATENT dict from `sample()`, so that I receive a `PIL.Image.Image` I can save, display, or pass downstream.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `vae_decode(vae, latent)` accepts the ComfyUI VAE object (from `CheckpointResult.vae`)
Pixel values are in the expected 0–255 uint8 range (image is not washed-out or clipped).
The function is importable as `from pycomfy import vae_decode` (re-exported from `__init__.py`).
`pycomfy/vae.py` is import-safe at module level in a CPU-only environment (no GPU
`uv run pytest` passes in CI (CPU-only, no GPU required).
Typecheck / lint passes (PEP 8, type hints on the public function signature).

userStories.title: pytest coverage for vae_decode
userStories.description: As a developer maintaining pycomfy, I want at least one pytest test that validates `vae_decode` produces a correct `PIL.Image` from a synthetic or mocked latent, so that regressions are caught in CI without needing a GPU or a real checkpoint.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The test imports `vae_decode` from `pycomfy` (public surface, not internals).
The test runs on CPU without loading a real checkpoint
All existing tests continue to pass (`uv run pytest`).

functionalRequirements.description: ** `vae_decode(vae: Any, latent: Any) -> PIL.Image.Image` — public function in
** The LATENT input follows the ComfyUI contract: a dict with a `"samples"` key
** The PIL image output must be RGB mode with pixel values in the 0–255 uint8
